title: rumex pulcher var. woodsii
intro: Rumex pulcher var. woodsii, also known as Wood's sorrel, is a variant of the common sorrel plant that is native to North America. It is a perennial herb with edible leaves and stems that are rich in vitamins and minerals, particularly ascorbic acid, also known as vitamin C. The medicinal properties of Wood's sorrel include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an antioxidant. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can provide health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress, improving immune function, and promoting wound healing.
